title: eriogonum suffrutescens
intro: Eriogonum suffrutescens, also known as Sulphur Flower Buckwheat, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Eriogonum suffrutescens can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping its flowers and leaves in hot water, and is sometimes used to support overall health and well-being.
